chapter_title.en: Chapter on the Legal Punishment for Slanderous Accusation

text: 5075 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ يُونُسَ بْنِ عَبْدِ اَلرَّحْمَنِ عَنْ بَعْضِ رِجَالِهِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: "كُلُّ بَالِغٍ مِنْ ذَكَرٍ أَوْ أُنْثَى اِفْتَرَى عَلَى صَغِيرٍ أَوْ كَبِيرٍ أَوْ ذَكَرٍ أَوْ أُنْثَى أَوْ مُسْلِمٍ أَوْ حُرٍّ أَوْ مَمْلُوكٍ فَعَلَيْهِ حَدُّ اَلْفِرْيَةِ وَ عَلَى غَيْرِ اَلْبَالِغِ حَدُّ اَلْأَدَبِ ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5075 - In the narration of Yunus ibn Abd Al-Rahman from some of his narrators from Abu Abdullah (as), he said: "Every adult, whether male or female, who falsely accuses a minor or an adult, a male or a female, a Muslim, a free person, or a slave, is subject to the prescribed punishment (hadd Al-firya).
And for one who is not yet an adult, the punishment is disciplinary (hadd Al-adab)."
